work.title: NYU Shanghai
work.subtitle: Learning Assistant
highlights: Worked as Learning Assistant for Calculus during Fall 2023 at Academic Resource Center, NYU Shanghai.

work.title: Data Intelligence and Social Computing Lab, Fudan University
work.subtitle: Lab Assistant
highlights: Constructed ~300K instruction data and fine-tuned DISC-LawLLM which is specialized in Chinese legal domain.
Built a retrieval module for DISC-LawLLM and constructed its database with 800+ Laws and 24K+ legal examinations.
Designed an evaluation framework from objective and subjective perspectives. DISC-LawLLM outperformed the base model by 23% and GPT-3.5 Turbo by 9%.

work.title: NYU Shanghai
work.subtitle: Learning Assistant
highlights: Worked as Learning Assistant for Calculus during Fall 2021 at Academic Resource Center, NYU Shanghai.

research.title: Efficiently Visualizing Large Graphs
research.subtitle: Deans' Undergraduate Research Fund (DURF)
highlights: Proposed SPLEE, a new graph embedding method based on Laplacian eigenmaps and shortest paths.
Proposed t-SGNE, a new dimension reduction method designed for graphs, improving runtime while maintaining visualization quality.
Proposed a new graph visualization method suitable for large graphs by combining SPLEE and t-SGNE.

projects.title: Scikit-learn
projects.subtitle: Open Source Development
projects.remarks: Python, numpy, scipy, scikit-learn, pytest, asv
highlights: Top #50 contributor to scikit-learn, with 62 merged pull requests, 2600 lines of addition and 1833 lines of deletion.

projects.title: Pandas
projects.subtitle: Open Source Development
highlights: Top #78 contributor to pandas, with 24 merged pull requests, 1297 lines of addition and 295 lines of deletion.

projects.title: YouTube Interface Customizer
projects.subtitle: Course Project
highlights: Built a Firefox extension that allows users to change color themes and hide, rearrange, or customize various elements of the YouTube interface.
Created the documentations of features and contribution guides, and released (self-distributed) v1.0 at Mozilla Add-ons.

projects.title: Inequality Process Simulation
projects.subtitle: Course Project
highlights: Simulated the inequality process via random transactions with certain transaction functions, and analyzed the effect of different transaction functions on the model.
Fitted the final distribution of wealth and found that the distribution of wealth in a real-world economic system fits the shape of a gamma or beta prime distribution.

projects.title: Gyro-Tower Simulation
projects.subtitle: Course Project
highlights: Simulated gyroscopes as networks of springs, and solved the system via differential equations and Euler's method.
Simulated horizontal stacks of gyroscopes, and found that they obeyed gyroscopic precession assuming a flexible middle axle.

projects.title: Simple SQL Optimizer
projects.subtitle: ByteDance 4th Youth Training Camp
highlights: Implemented an SQL parser that accepts various operators including Filter, GroupBy, Join, etc. to form an abstract syntax tree.
Implemented rule-based predicate pushdown, limit pushdown, and column pruning to optimize the abstract syntax tree.
Implemented cost-based join reordering to optimize the abstract syntax tree.
